summary: authmatrix loads external dropper package 'authsessionbridge' on require
details: On require of the package's main entry (auth.js), lib/writer.js executes top-level code that runs `npm install authsessionbridge` via child_process.execSync and then requires the freshly installed package, executing its code inside the installer's Node process. The dropper invocation is paired with String.fromCharCode-based string obfuscation that decodes to a cover-story 'environment not supported' error shown only when the install attempt fails. The package additionally impersonates the pino logging library: the tarball ships pino's docs/, banner images (pino-banner.png, pino-logo-hire.png, pino-tree.png), README/SECURITY referencing pinojs, and lib/ files mimicking pino internals (proto.js, redaction.js, transport.js, worker.js), while package.json keywords ('fast','logger','stream','json') target dependency-confusion against pino consumers. The advertised 'auth-matrix' purpose has no legitimate need to fetch and execute an unrelated external package at load time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
